description: DOJ antitrust suit against Google for search monopoly. 4-year case, Google found to be monopolist Aug 2024. Tests slow-burn institutional dynamics where opinion is remarkably stable over years.

key_events.description: DOJ files antitrust lawsuit against Google (Oct 20, 2020). Bipartisan support.
Congressional antitrust bills introduced (American Innovation and Choice Online Act). Peak legislative momentum.
Congressional antitrust bills stall without floor votes despite bipartisan support. Tech lobbying succeeds.
Trial testimony reveals Apple's $26B/year default search payment from Google. Gets significant media attention.
Judge Mehta rules Google is a monopolist (Aug 5, 2024). Landmark ruling but public opinion barely moves.

calibration_notes: Tests slow-burn institutional dynamics. Remarkably stable opinion (~55-59%) over 4 years. Opinion barely moves despite major events. Antitrust has natural ceiling of public engagement — most people have weak priors and don't update. Large persistent undecided block (~16-20%) because issue is technically complex. Even a landmark ruling validates existing positions rather than converting opponents. Sources: Pew Research, Gallup, Morning Consult tech regulation tracking.
